title: RHCopywriterBrief
description: Input brief for the Red Hat Copywriter Skill v1.1.0. Authoritative machine-readable input contract. Field names and enums align with skill.md v1.1.0 and templates/copy-brief.md v1.1.0.

content_type.description: Determines which workflow the skill invokes. partner_value_prop → workflows/partner-value-prop.md (single output block). summit_prep → workflows/summit-prep.md (three sequential outputs: partner value prop, Before You Go narrative, announcement blurb).

audience.description: Primary audience segment. Controls tone register, vocabulary, and assumed-knowledge level per references/audience-profiles.md. technical_decision_maker: IT architects, platform engineers, CTOs. business_executive: C-suite, CFO, COO, VP of IT. partner_sales: channel partners, ISV/GSI partners, co-sell partner SEs and AMs.

product_or_initiative.description: The Red Hat product, product portfolio, or joint initiative that is the subject of the copy. Must use approved product names from references/brand-and-tone-notes.md on first reference.

key_messages.description: Specific claims or differentiators the copy must communicate, in priority order. Index 0 is the highest-priority message. Each message should be a specific, factual claim. Metrics should include the source in parentheses.

tone_variant.description: Register and style for the generated copy. standard: professional and direct, default for partner sales and general PMM content. technical: precise, practitioner-facing. executive: strategic and concise, conclusion-first. conversational: approachable, for event previews and announcement blurbs.

output_format.description: Controls the structure of the Body Copy section. prose: flowing paragraphs (3–5). bullets: bulleted list (4–7 items). slide-ready: short labelled blocks for direct paste into presentation slides.

partner_name.description: Full legal or trading name of the partner organisation. When present, activates partner-first framing (F-01, F-02) throughout all output. If absent and content_type is partner-oriented, the skill inserts [PARTNER_NAME] placeholders.

word_limit.description: Optional Body Copy word count target. The skill generates output within ±10% of this value. Applies to Body Copy only; does not affect Headline or CTA. Defaults to workflow-specific range if omitted.

call_to_action.description: Optional explicit CTA text used verbatim in every output block's CTA section. Use when the GTM team has already approved a specific URL, stand reference, or action verb. When omitted, the skill derives a CTA from key_messages.

event_metadata.description: Optional event-specific metadata. Applies only when content_type is summit_prep. Silently ignored for other content types. When present, substitutes real event values for placeholder strings in Before You Go and announcement blurb outputs.

event_name.description: Full name of the event. Required when event_metadata block is present. Example: 'Red Hat Summit 2026'.

event_date.description: ISO 8601 date or date range. Examples: '2026-05-06' or '2026-05-06/2026-05-08'.

event_location.description: City, venue name, or 'Virtual'. Example: 'Boston, MA — Boston Convention and Exhibition Center'.

booth_reference.description: Partner booth or stand number. Example: 'Booth H3-420'. If omitted, [BOOTH_REFERENCE] is inserted in output and listed in unresolved_placeholders.

language.description: BCP 47 language tag for the requested output language. Defaults to 'en' (English). v1.1 supports English only. Other values are accepted without validation error; the skill generates English output and includes a Confidence Note advisory directing the operator to docs/localisation-roadmap.md.
